name: Secure Agent Mesh — SMILE Planner + Devil's Advocate
description: A two-agent system where Agent A builds a constraint-aware SMILE implementation plan and Agent B independently critiques it using real case studies. The orchestrator enforces A2A discovery, schema validation between agents, and multi-layer security. Neither agent produces a useful result alone — the value comes from the adversarial collaboration.

supportedInterfaces.comment: Orchestrator reads planner.json and validator.json for agent discovery, then chains agents via subprocess pipes with schema validation at every boundary.

agents.role: Builds a phased SMILE implementation roadmap from LPI methodology data
Critiques the plan against real case studies — finds what will fail before you act on it

security.inputSanitization: Regex-based injection filter on all user inputs before any agent is invoked
security.interAgentValidation: JSON schema check on plan payload before passing from Agent A to Agent B
security.revalidation: Agent B re-sanitizes all string fields from the plan even when received from Agent A
security.dosProtection: 500-char input cap, 180s LLM timeout, 300s subprocess timeout
